expectations.text: F1: Flags routes.ts importing drizzle-orm/db schema and querying the DB directly inside the route handler (presentation touching infrastructure)
expectations.evidence: Finding #1: 'Route handler queries Drizzle directly ... This is presentation-layer code importing infrastructure directly — the exact anti-pattern in examples.md #1', cites dependency-cruiser rule 'handlers-never-touch-the-db'

expectations.text: F2: Flags the endpoint-URL uniqueness check as business logic misplaced in the route handler
expectations.evidence: Finding #1: 'the duplicate-URL check here (existing.some(...)) is a business rule (uniqueness invariant), and it's sitting in the transport layer instead of the domain/application layer'

expectations.text: F3: Flags claimDueRetries' backoff/MAX_ATTEMPTS logic as a domain rule embedded in the repository/infrastructure layer
expectations.evidence: Finding #6: 'Business rule (retry eligibility / backoff / dead-lettering) implemented inside the repository ... Retry-eligibility and backoff computation is exactly this kind of workflow decision — it's a domain invariant... not a data-access concern'

expectations.text: F4: Flags repository methods returning raw Drizzle rows outward with no row->entity/DTO mapping
expectations.evidence: Finding #7: 'Repository returns raw Drizzle rows outward instead of mapping to domain entities/DTOs' — also independently catches that the leaked row round-trips the `secret` column back to the client

expectations.text: F5: Flags service.ts constructing a concrete OpenAI client directly instead of going through the LLMProvider port/container
expectations.evidence: Finding #4: 'Application service owns a concrete OpenAI client instead of depending on an LLMProvider port ... This is the clearest violation in the module', cites SKILL.md tool->layer mapping verbatim

extra_findings_beyond_seed: Services/repositories constructed outside the composition root (container.ts) instead of injected
Route handler hand-rolls req.params/req.body casts instead of schema-first Zod params validation
No domain layer / repository interface exists for the module at all (WebhooksService depends on the concrete class, not a port)
